trivia_bullets.text: Cloud Manta's canonical type_line is 'Creature — Fish' (verified via Scryfall API). NOTE: the vision pass tagged this card with 'creature-beast' in tags_filter — this is incorrect per the Scryfall type_line. Cloud Manta is a Fish, not a Beast. It is one of the few aerial Fish in the game, representing Zendikar's sky-sailing fauna adapted to the plane's floating landmasses.

Flavor text: 'When Emeria's worshippers learned that she was no more than a twisted memory of Emrakul, they abandoned their temples to the god. The deserted shrines now serve as breeding grounds for the mantas.' This encodes BFZ's central lore disclosure: the goddess Emeria (venerated by the kor as wind deity, by the merfolk as sky-realm goddess) was a corrupted racial memory of the Eldrazi titan Emrakul — Emrakul's vast silhouette blotting the sky long ago was mythologized into a divine protector. When the truth emerged in BFZ, Emeria religion collapsed.
sources: MTG Wiki: Emeria (via WebSearch snippet from mtg.fandom.com/wiki/Emeria)

trivia_bullets.text: Cloud Manta is a rare BFZ non-Eldrazi common that carries set-story weight entirely through flavor text, while its body ({3}{U} for a 3/2 Flying Fish with no other abilities) sits below the curve for both Limited and Commander. The card uses the Eldrazi revelation to reframe ordinary wildlife: the mantas colonize abandoned sacred sites not because they are corrupted, but because the human structures have been vacated.

EDHREC rank 26,996 places Cloud Manta at the bottom of the BFZ card pool in Commander inclusion. It has no reprint history (Scryfall: reprint: false) and no format-specific community use case. Its value to the graph is primarily as a flavor-text carrier for the Emeria/Emrakul revelation.
